name: High-p16 T3 4-week CTx inconclusive exploratory result
comparator: Untreated control within high-p16 T3 subgroup
arm_details: Highest tertile of baseline T cell p16_variant 5 mRNA levels; article notes limited power because the T3 subgroup had about 10 participants per study arm.

effect.raw_text: In the high-p16 T3 subgroup, serum CTx showed a -18% relative-to-control decrease at 4 weeks; P=0.053.

analysis.comparison: D+Q versus untreated control in T3 subgroup
analysis.effect_note: Exploratory subgroup effect was close to, but above, the conventional P<0.05 threshold.

provenance.note: Full-text exploratory subgroup paragraph and figure caption provide the effect, p-value, subgroup definition, and T3 sample size at 4 weeks.

statement: In the exploratory high-p16 T3 subgroup, the 4-week CTx decrease was directionally similar to the 2-week signal but statistically inconclusive.
caveats: Exploratory subgroup analysis; do not generalize to the overall randomized comparison.
This result contrasts with the null overall primary CTx result at 20 weeks.
The reported P=0.053 should not be represented as a confirmed effect.
